intro: C++ delivers complete structural and runtime control over native hardware operations, combining static typing with zero-overhead resource abstractions. TypeScript overlays a flexible structural type layer on top of JavaScript, adding compile-time checks while retaining compatibility with dynamic web runtimes. Engineers move from C++ to TypeScript to build scalable, type-safe web and cloud applications without sacrificing the fast iteration cycles of a modern scripting language.

diffs.from: Static, nominal type system resolved at compile time to map exact memory layouts and structural alignments.
diffs.to: Static, structural type configuration evaluating object shapes, supporting unions, intersections, and mapped types.

diffs.from: Complex multi-layered syntax involving explicit header boundaries, namespaces, and template constraints.
diffs.to: Modern, readable syntax extending standard ECMAScript with explicit type annotations, interfaces, and enums.

diffs.from: Strict, explicit tracking architectures utilizing stack mechanics and RAII smart pointer frameworks.
diffs.to: Abstracted runtime memory management handled entirely by the host platform's garbage collector.

diffs.from: Native multi-core concurrency using system threads, atomics, and low-level synchronization variables.
diffs.to: Event-driven asynchronous flows powered by an event loop, utilizing `Promises` and `async/await` syntax.

diffs.from: The Standard Template Library (STL), providing precise, performance-tuned system algorithms and collection containers.
diffs.to: Standard ECMAScript APIs combined with Node.js or browser environment layers, focusing on I/O and text streams.

examples.title: Interface and Shape Definition
examples.description: Replacing nominal struct models with flexible, compile-verified structural interfaces.

examples.title: Generic Collection Wrappers
examples.description: Migrating from template specialization logic to runtime structural generic interfaces.

tips.title: Avoid Overusing the Any Escape Hatch
tips.body: Do not default variables to `any` just to bypass compiler diagnostics. Use the explicit `unknown` type when input structures are unverified, forcing safe runtime type guards.

tips.title: Leverage Structural, Not Nominal Matching
tips.body: Remember that TypeScript evaluates type compatibility by checking if object properties match, rather than relying strictly on named class inheritances.

tips.title: Enable Strict Compilation Properties
tips.body: Ensure `strict: true` is enabled in your `tsconfig.json` file to turn on vital safety features, such as strict null validation checks across your code.

tips.title: Use Type Unions Instead of Enum Cascades
tips.body: Instead of defining heavy runtime `enum` objects, use clean literal string union combinations (e.g., `'read' | 'write'`) to enforce type constraints at compile time.
